originalTitle: Women secretly filmed, then ridiculed and abused online
title: Being Kind and Safe Online: A Story from Kenya and Ghana!
content:
Hi there, amazing kids of Africa and everywhere! It's your friendly news reporter here, ready to share an important story with you. Today, we're going to talk about something that happened to some grown-up ladies in two special countries in Africa: Kenya and Ghana. It's a story about being kind, asking permission, and staying safe online. Let's learn together! Imagine your big sister or your auntie. Well, this story is about ladies just like them. They live in countries called Kenya and Ghana. Do you know where Kenya and Ghana are? They are right here on our big continent of Africa! Kenya is in East Africa, known for its amazing wildlife like giraffes and elephants, and beautiful beaches. Ghana is in West Africa, famous for its music, rich history, and friendly people. These ladies are part of our African family. One day, these ladies met a man. He was from a country very, very far away, called Russia. That's across the ocean! The man seemed friendly. He talked to them, and he took some videos with his camera or phone. Now, taking videos can be fun, right? We take videos of our friends playing, or our family celebrating. But there's a very important rule we all need to remember when we take pictures or videos of other people.

Asking for Permission is Super Important!

This is the most important part of our story, friends. Imagine someone takes a picture of you while you're eating your lunch, and then they show it to *everyone* without asking you first. How would that make you feel? Maybe a little shy? Or maybe even a bit upset? That's because they didn't ask your permission. Permission means saying 'Yes, it's okay!' or 'No, please don't.' It's like asking, 'Can I borrow your crayon?' or 'Can I play with your ball?' The grown-up ladies in Kenya and Ghana didn't know what the man would do with their videos. He took their videos, but then, he shared them on the internet for lots and lots of people to see, without asking them if it was okay. This made the ladies feel very, very sad and uncomfortable. It's like someone telling everyone your secret without you saying it was fine. *Did you know?* Asking for permission shows respect! It means you care about how the other person feels.

What is the Internet?

Now, let's talk about the 'internet.' Have you heard of it? It's like a giant, invisible playground where people from all over the world can share things. They share pictures, videos, stories, and messages. It's an amazing place where we can learn new things and talk to friends who live far away. But, just like any playground, we need to be careful on the internet. Not everything on the internet is good or kind. *Did you know?* The internet connects billions of people! That's like everyone in Africa, and then some, all linked together!

When Words Hurt: Being Kind Online

The man shared the videos of the ladies on the internet. And sadly, some people who saw the videos were not very kind. They wrote mean words about the ladies. This is called 'ridiculing' or 'abusing' them online. It means they were making fun of them and saying hurtful things. Imagine someone making fun of your drawing, or the way you play. How would that make you feel? Probably sad or angry, right? Words have power, friends. Kind words can make someone smile and feel happy. Unkind words can make someone feel very, very small and hurt their feelings a lot, even if those words are just on a screen. When the ladies saw these unkind words, they felt even worse. They were already sad that their videos were shared without asking, and then people were mean about it too. *Did you know?* What you put on the internet can stay there for a very long time, like a footprint in the sand! So always think before you post or type.

Our Superpower: Being Respectful and Safe

So, what can we learn from this story? We have a superpower inside us, and that superpower is called RESPECT. Respect means treating others the way you want to be treated. Here are some super important lessons for all of us: 1. **Always Ask Permission:** Before you take a picture or video of someone, always ask them, 'Is it okay if I take your picture?' or 'Can I film this?' If they say no, then that's okay, you respect their choice. If someone takes a picture or video of you and you don't like it, tell them kindly to stop or to delete it. 2. **Be Kind with Your Words:** Whether you're talking in person or typing on a phone or computer, always choose kind words. If you see someone being unkind online, don't join in. Instead, you can ignore it or tell a grown-up. We want the internet to be a friendly place for everyone. 3. **Talk to a Trusted Grown-Up:** If anything on the internet makes you feel scared, sad, or confused, quickly go and tell a grown-up you trust. This could be your parent, your teacher, your auntie, or an older sibling. They can help you understand and stay safe. It's never a good idea to keep scary or upsetting things a secret online. *Did you know?* There are special people called 'digital citizens' who help make the internet a better and safer place for everyone by being kind and responsible! You can be a digital citizen too!

Helping Our Friends in Africa

People are learning about what happened to these ladies in Kenya and Ghana. Grown-ups are talking about how we can make sure this doesn't happen again. They are working to help the ladies who felt sad and to teach everyone about respecting privacy online. Privacy means keeping some things just for yourself, or only sharing them with people you choose. It's a reminder that we all need to look out for each other, both in real life and online. We want our African communities, and communities all over the world, to be places where everyone feels safe, respected, and happy.

Let's Be Super Online Explorers!

Thank you for listening to this important story, my wonderful friends. Remember, the internet can be a fantastic place for learning and fun, but we must use it wisely and kindly. Always ask permission, always be kind with your words, and always tell a trusted grown-up if you see or feel something that makes you worried online. Let's promise to be super online explorers, always using our superpowers of respect and kindness! Until next time, stay safe and keep smiling!
summary: Grown-up ladies in Kenya and Ghana were filmed by a man and their videos were shared online without their permission, making them feel sad and upset when people said unkind things.
whyMatters: It's super important to always ask permission before taking or sharing pictures and videos of anyone, and to remember that kind words make people happy, while unkind words can hurt, especially when shared on the internet.

originalTitle: France calls IOC sex testing a ‘step backwards’ while Trump praises move
title: New Rules for Olympic Sports!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends! Your news reporter from Africa here, bringing you some exciting news! Guess what? There's some news from the big world of sports! It's about the Olympic Games, where super athletes from all over the world run, jump, and swim. The grown-ups who help organize these games made some new rules, and we're going to talk about them simply so everyone can understand.

The Amazing Olympic Games!

Do you know about the Olympic Games? They are super, super special! Athletes, who are like grown-up sports stars, come from every corner of the world. They meet in one place to show off their amazing talents. They run very, very fast, jump very, very high, and swim super, super quick! They compete to see who is the best at different sports, like running races, long jumps, and swimming races. It's like a giant sports day, but for the whole world! Everyone watches and cheers for their favorite athletes. **Did you know?** The Olympic Games happen only every four years! It's a very old tradition that has been going on for a very long time!

Why Do We Need Rules in Sports?

Imagine playing a game with your friends without any rules. It might get a bit messy, right? Maybe someone runs off with the ball, or someone says it's their turn when it isn't! Rules are super important! They help everyone play fair. They make sure everyone has a good, equal chance to play and win. Rules help us know what is allowed and what is not. They make sports fun and safe for everyone! No one wants to play a game that isn't fair. The grown-ups who organize the Olympics make rules for all the games. These rules help all the athletes play their best and be fair to each other.

Boys and Girls Play Together, But Also Apart

When you play at school, sometimes boys and girls play together in the same games. You might play tag or chase together, and that's lots of fun! But sometimes, in sports like football or running races, boys and girls have separate teams. Have you ever wondered why? This is because, sometimes, boys and girls can be a bit different physically. As they grow up, boys often grow up to be stronger and faster than girls in some sports like wrestling or sprinting. This is just how our bodies are made! It's not better or worse, just different! And that's totally okay! It means we can all find sports where we can shine. **Did you know?** Some animals, like big lions and lionesses, also have differences between the males and females! The male lions often have big manes, and the lionesses are often faster hunters!

A New Rule for Girls' Sports at the Olympics

Now, let's talk about the new rule for the Olympic Games. It's about girls' sports, like running races or swimming races for girls. The people who make the rules for the Olympics talked a lot and then said something new. They said that in girls' sports, only athletes who were born as girls can play. This means if someone was born a boy, but now feels like a girl and wants to play in girls' teams, they can't play in the girls' competitions at the Olympics anymore. This is a big change, and it's a new rule for some of the athletes who love to play.

Why Did They Make This New Rule?

The grown-ups who made this rule thought very, very hard about what is fair. They want to make sure that all the girls who play in girls' sports have a truly fair chance to win. They worried that if someone who was born a boy played in girls' sports, it might not be fair to the girls who were born girls. This is because, as we said, boys can sometimes be stronger or faster, even if they now feel like girls. So, they changed the rule to try and make sports as fair as possible for everyone who was born a girl. They want every girl to have an equal opportunity to win medals and be celebrated. **Did you know?** Being fair means treating everyone equally and giving them the same chances! It's like sharing your toys so everyone gets a turn.

What Does This Mean for Athletes?

For many girl athletes, nothing much changes. They will still train super hard and play their favorite sports, just like before. But for some athletes who were born boys but feel like girls, it means they cannot play in the girls' competitions at the Olympics. This might make some athletes sad, because they love to play sports and want to be part of the games. It's a tricky situation, and grown-ups are still talking about it a lot. Everyone wants to find the best way for sports to be fair and fun for everyone. The most important thing is that everyone gets to play sports and have fun, even if the rules change sometimes. There are many other wonderful ways to be involved in sports!

Grown-ups Have Different Ideas

When a big rule changes, like this one, not everyone agrees right away. That's okay! It's normal for grown-ups to have different ideas and opinions. Some grown-ups think this new rule is a very good idea. They believe it will help make girls' sports super fair and protect the chances of girls who were born girls. Other grown-ups think it's not such a good idea. They believe that everyone should be able to play in the team or group they feel most comfortable with, no matter what. It shows that even grown-ups can have different ideas about what is fair and what is best for everyone. And they keep talking to each other to understand different points of view. **Did you know?** It's okay to have different ideas, as long as we are kind and listen to each other with respect! We can learn a lot from different thoughts.

What Matters Most in Sports?

No matter what the rules are, sports are about many wonderful things. They are not just about winning! They are about trying your very best, every single time you play. It's about the effort you put in! They are about being a good friend and a good teammate. Cheering for others and helping your friends is very important. They are about having fun and being healthy! Running and playing makes our bodies strong and our minds happy. They are about cheering for everyone, no matter who wins or loses. Being a good sport means being kind to others, even if they win or lose against you. And they are about respecting all the players and all the rules. So, let's keep playing, keep smiling, and keep being good sports! So, that's the news about the new Olympic rules for girls' sports. It's all about trying to make things fair for everyone so that every athlete has a good chance. Remember to always be kind to your friends, play fair, and have lots of fun! See you next time with more exciting news from Africa!
summary: The Olympic Games have a new rule: only athletes born as girls can play in girls' sports, to make sure everyone has a fair chance.
whyMatters: This rule matters because it changes who can play in girls' sports at the Olympics, aiming to keep sports fair for all girls, even though some grown-ups have different ideas about it.

originalTitle: UN human rights chief calls on US to conclude probe into Iran school strike
title: Caring for Children Everywhere!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends! I'm your special news reporter, bringing you important stories from our big, beautiful world, all the way from Africa! Today, we're talking about something very important: keeping children safe. Everyone wants children to be safe, right?

A Sad Story From Far Away

Sometimes, far, far away from us, sad things can happen. A kind grown-up named Mr. Volker Türk shared a sad story with us. He works for a very big group called the United Nations. Think of it like a huge team of countries that work together to make the world a better place. Mr. Türk told us about a school in a country called Iran. Something bad happened there. Many children and grown-ups were hurt, and some even died. This made many people around the world very, very sad. Did you know? The United Nations has many helpers all over the world. They try to stop bad things from happening and help people when they are in trouble. They work for peace!

Who is Mr. Volker Türk?

Mr. Türk is like a super helper for all people. His job is to make sure everyone's 'human rights' are looked after. What are human rights? They are like special rules that say everyone should be treated fairly and be safe. You have human rights too! You have the right to go to school, the right to play, the right to eat yummy food, and the right to be safe. Mr. Türk wants to make sure all children have these rights, no matter where they live.

What Does "Probe" Mean?

When something sad like this happens, grown-ups want to find out why. Mr. Türk said it's important to 'probe' what happened. 'Probe' is a big word, but it just means to look very, very carefully, like a detective looking for clues! They want to understand everything. They want to find out who did it and why. This is important so that they can try to stop it from happening again. Finding answers helps everyone.

Why Is It Important to Find Out?

It's super important to find out exactly what happened at that school. Imagine if someone broke your favorite toy. You would want to know why, right? And you'd want to make sure it doesn't get broken again. It's the same for big, sad things in the world. Finding out the truth helps families understand. It also helps grown-ups learn how to keep schools and children much safer in the future. Every child deserves to feel safe at school, at home, and everywhere they go. Did you know? There's a special day every year called Universal Children's Day! It's a day to remember that all children everywhere have special rights and deserve to be happy and safe.

Who Needs to Help "Probe"?

Mr. Türk has asked a country called the United States to help find out the truth. They need to finish looking for all the clues and tell everyone what they found. When many grown-ups and many countries work together, it's easier to find answers and make things right. Teamwork is super important, even for big world problems! Everyone working together makes the world a better place for children.

Why This Matters to All Children

This story might be from far away, but it matters to all of us. Why? Because we all want a world where children can laugh, play, and learn without any fear. Just like you love going to school here in Africa, children in Iran and everywhere else want to go to school and feel safe. When one child is hurt, it makes all kind-hearted people sad. We learn to care about others, no matter where they live. We want a world where schools are always safe places for learning and fun. Did you know? There are over 2 billion children in the world! That's a lot of friends to share our planet with, and we want all of them to be happy and safe. We are all part of one big global family.

What We Hope For

So, what do we hope for? We hope that the grown-ups will find all the answers very soon. We hope they will learn how to make sure schools are safe for every child, every day. And we hope that all children, in Africa and all around the world, can grow up healthy, happy, and safe. Let's all wish for peace and safety for children everywhere! Thank you for listening, my wonderful news explorers!
summary: Mr. Volker Türk from the United Nations wants grown-ups to find out why a school was hurt in a far-off country, so all children can be safe.
whyMatters: It is important because every child in the world deserves to be safe at school and to play without fear, and we need to understand what happened to make sure it doesn't happen again and keep all children safe.
